name: PHYS_MEM_MAP_ZwSection_NtCurrentProcess_SuperBMC_shape

byte_sequences.hex: 48 C7 C2 FF FF FF FF 48 8B 8C 24 ?? ?? 00 00 FF 15

notes: PHYS_MEM_MAP via the ZwOpenSection(\Device\PhysicalMemory, SECTION_ALL_ACCESS=0x000F001F) + ObReferenceObjectByHandle + ZwMapViewOfSection(ProcessHandle=-1=NtCurrentProcess) primitive shape, the Cuckoo/Cerrudo PhysicalMemory-into-user-space pattern. Sequence (1) is the distinguishing fingerprint: 'mov rdx, 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF (NtCurrentProcess); mov rcx, [SectionHandle on stack]; call cs:ZwMapViewOfSection' — rdx=-1 as the ProcessHandle argument to ZwMapViewOfSection maps physical memory directly into the caller's user-mode address space. Sequence (2) is the post-call NTSTATUS check 'call [imp_X]; mov [rsp+FC],eax; cmp dword [rsp+FC],0; jge' wrapping each Zw* call — a strong cluster anchor in this driver family. Sequence (3) is the HANDLE_DUP-shape ObReferenceObjectByHandle with wildcard DesiredAccess=0 ('xor r9d,r9d (KernelMode); xor r8d,r8d (ObjectType=any); xor edx,edx (DesiredAccess=0); mov rcx,[Handle]; call'). Sequence (4) is the caller-controlled HalTranslateBusAddress prologue 'mov edx,[user_BusNumber]; mov ecx,[user_InterfaceType]; ...; call' — BUS_ADDR_TRANSLATE primitive anchor. Sequence (5) is the SECTION_ALL_ACCESS=0x000F001F immediate 'mov edx, 1Fh000Fh' that precedes the ZwOpenSection call on PhysicalMemory. Import gate requires the ZwSection+ObRef+HalTransBus+IoCreateDevice (non-secure) sextet and forbids MmMapIoSpace family (this driver explicitly avoids the more common physmem-mapping route, which is the distinguishing fingerprint vs RTCore64/cpuz/AsrDrv shapes), as well as standard hardening imports (ProbeForRead/Write, SeAccessCheck, SePrivilegeCheck, ExGetPreviousMode, IoCreateDeviceSecure) and Wdf/Flt frameworks. Reference: superbmc.sys (Super Micro BMC management) at mj14=0x12ea0, device_type=0x8010, 6 IOCTLs (0x80102208/0x20/0x38/0x40/0x48/0x60), open-gate; PhysMem map at site 0x14cf3 via IOCTL 0x80102220 path, twin HalTranslateBusAddress calls at 0x14bb5/0x14be7 via IOCTL 0x80102238 path, HANDLE_DUP-shape ObRefByHandle at 0x12cb8 in the create-context path. Self-test on reference: seq1=1, seq2=3, seq3=2, seq4=2, seq5=2 (10 total occurrences). The ZwSection-PhysMem path is rarely seen in BYOVD corpus — most weaponizable physmem mappers use MmMapIoSpace, making this Zw-path family small but distinctive.
